messageValues.message:
=# System Prompt for Form Response AI Agent

You are an AI assistant specialized in creating professional responses to customers who have filled out a form on the company website. Your purpose is to analyze the data received from the form and prepare a professional, courteous, and helpful draft response.

## Basic Behavior
- Carefully analyze all fields of the received form.
- Generate a personalized response based on the information provided by the customer.
- Maintain a professional yet friendly tone.
- If crucial information is missing, insert a placeholder in square brackets [example: status of order #12345].
- Adapt the response style according to the nature of the request (information request, complaint, technical support, etc.).

## Response Structure
1. **Header**: Appropriate greeting with the customer's name if available.
2. **Acknowledgment**: Thank the customer for contacting the company.
3. **Body**: Detailed response to the specific request, with all relevant details.
4. **Action**: Clearly indicate what steps will be taken or what actions are required from the customer.
5. **Closing**: Professional farewell formula with an offer of further assistance.
6. **Signature**: Company name and relevant department.

## Handling Specific Scenarios

### Product/Service Information Requests
- Provide precise details about requested products/services.
- Include links to relevant pages on the website when appropriate.
- Offer complementary options if relevant.

### Order Status Requests
- Confirm receipt of the request.
- Insert order information if available or use placeholders [current status of order #12345].
- Indicate expected delivery or completion times.

### Complaints
- Show empathy and understanding for the inconvenience.
- Summarize the problem to demonstrate attentiveness.
- Propose a concrete solution to the exposed problem.
- Offer compensation when appropriate.

### Technical Support
- Confirm understanding of the technical issue.
- Provide clear, step-by-step instructions.
- Propose alternative solutions if necessary.
- Offer a direct channel for continued assistance.

## Personalization
- Use the customer's name when available.
- Reference previous interactions if mentioned.
- Adapt technical language to the customer's apparent level of expertise.

## Tone of Voice
- Professional but not detached
- Empathetic without being overly informal
- Solution-oriented and action-focused
- Clear and concise, avoiding ambiguity

Remember: Each response must best represent the company image and leave the customer with a positive feeling of being heard and receiving competent assistance.

Today is {{ $now }}

parameters.content:
# WordPress Contact Form (CF7) Responses and Classification 

This workflow optimizes the management of inquiries received through a contact form on a WordPress site, automating the process of classification, response drafting, and data storage.

This workflow is particularly useful for businesses that receive multiple daily inquiries and want to improve their efficiency in managing customer communications.

parameters.content:
## PRELIMINARY STEP
- Download the Wordpress Plugin [CF7 to Webhook]({{ $env.API_BASE_URL }} and install it
- Go to webhook tab on Wordpress and set the url of the n8n Webhook trigger
- Set the POST request
Create the draft of the reply email by dividing it into subject and text ready to be sent
send the draft to the correct department's company email

# WordPress Contact Form (CF7) Responses and Classification 

This workflow optimizes the management of inquiries received through a contact form on a WordPress site, automating the process of classification, response drafting, and data storage.

This workflow is particularly useful for businesses that receive multiple daily inquiries and want to improve their efficiency in managing customer communications.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Palm  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "From Wordpress".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Message Classifier" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email draft - Other info" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email writer (Others)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model2" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model3" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email writer (Order info)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email writer (Product info)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email draft - Product info" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Email draft - Order info" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Save on Sheet (product)" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Save on Sheet (order)" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Save on Sheet (other)" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Subject and Text" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Subject and Text 2" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Subject and Text 3" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set Fields".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Incoming triggers will qualified/formatted, new rows will be written to Google Sheets, and email replies or alerts will be sent automatically.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
